q.en: What is the fastest living thing on land?
q.zh: 陆地上最快的生物是什么？

explain.en: The book says the cheetah, which runs up to 113 kph, 'is the fastest living thing on land.'
explain.zh: 书中说，猎豹奔跑速度可达每小时113公里，是“陆地上最快的生物”。

q.en: In the book, what does 'hydroplaning' mean for a boat?
q.zh: 在本书中，船的“hydroplaning”（滑水）是什么意思？

options.en: A boat skimming over the surface and barely touching the water
options.zh: 船贴着水面滑行、几乎不接触水

options.en: A plane landing on water according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 飞机在水面降落（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book explains hydroplaning means modern racing boats 'barely touch the water as they skim over the surface.'
explain.zh: 书中解释，滑水是指现代赛艇“贴着水面滑行，几乎不接触水”。

q.en: Which of these living things is the fastest?
q.zh: 以下哪种生物速度最快？

options.en: A sailfish according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 旗鱼（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says the peregrine falcon 'reaches speeds of 435 kph' diving—faster than cheetahs (113 kph) and sailfish (113 kph).
explain.zh: 书中说游隼俯冲时“速度可达每小时435公里”，快于猎豹（113公里）和旗鱼（113公里）。

q.en: What was the first recognized land-speed record for an automobile in 1898?
q.zh: 1898年公认的第一项汽车陆地速度纪录是多少？

options.en: 1,228 kph (763 mph) according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 每小时1228公里（763英里）（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: 63.13 kph (39.24 mph) by an electric car
options.zh: 电动汽车创造的每小时63.13公里（39.24英里）

explain.en: The book says the first recognized automobile land-speed record, set in 1898, was '63.13 kph' by an electric motor.
explain.zh: 书中说，1898年创造的第一项公认的汽车陆地速度纪录是由电动机驱动的“每小时63.13公里”。

q.en: Why does the author mention that light moves at 299,338 km per second?
q.zh: 作者为什么要提到光每秒移动299,338公里？

options.en: To give an example of something measured in very large units over a very short time
options.zh: 为了举例说明某些事物使用很大的距离单位和很短的时间单位来计量

options.en: To show that light is slow
To explain how a car works according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了解释汽车如何运作（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The author uses light as the fastest thing in the universe to show that faster objects are measured in larger distance units over shorter time.
explain.zh: 作者以光作为宇宙中速度最快的事物，说明越快的物体越用更大的距离单位和更短的时间单位来计量。

q.en: Who was the first human to go faster than the speed of sound on land?
q.zh: 谁是第一个在陆地上超过音速的人？

options.en: A bullet-train driver according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 子弹头列车驾驶员（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Andy Green in the Thrust SSC

explain.en: The book says in October 1997 Andy Green 'became the first human to go faster than the speed of sound in a land vehicle' (1,228 kph).
explain.zh: 书中说，1997年10月，安迪·格林“成为第一个在陆地车辆上超过音速的人”（每小时1228公里）。

q.en: Why did early bicycles have a problem going fast?
q.zh: 早期的自行车为什么难以提速？

options.en: Air resistance pushed back harder as speed increased
options.zh: 速度越快，空气阻力推得越厉害

options.en: The riders got too tired according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 骑行者太累了（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book explains, 'As we ride faster, the air seems to push back harder'—this air resistance slows cyclists.
explain.zh: 书中解释：“我们骑得越快，空气似乎推得越厉害”——这种空气阻力使骑行者变慢。

q.en: What is the main topic of the book?

options.en: Why cheetahs are slow according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为什么猎豹很慢（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: Speed—how it is measured and how different things move fast
options.zh: 速度——它如何被衡量，以及不同事物如何快速移动

explain.en: The book compares how speed is measured and how living things, vehicles, and even light move at different speeds.
explain.zh: 本书比较了速度如何被衡量，以及生物、交通工具甚至光以不同速度运动。

q.en: About how fast can a magnetic 'bullet train' travel?
q.zh: 磁悬浮“子弹头列车”大约能跑多快？

options.zh: 目前已经超过每小时800公里

explain.en: The book says modern bullet trains 'now travel at speeds of nearly 320 kilometers per hour.'
explain.zh: 书中说，现代子弹头列车“目前的时速接近320公里”。

q.en: What does the author say about the future of human speed?
q.zh: 关于人类速度的未来，作者说了什么？

options.en: Humans will keep finding new ways to go faster, even toward the speed of light
options.zh: 人类会不断找到提速的新方法，甚至接近光速

options.en: Speed records are finished according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 速度纪录已经到头了（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The conclusion says humans 'will continue to find ways to go faster' and one day may travel at the speed of light.
explain.zh: 结论说，人类“将继续找到提速的方法”，有朝一日或许能以光速旅行。

prompt.en: Choose one vehicle from the book (bicycle, boat, train, automobile, or aircraft) and explain how people made it go faster over time.
prompt.zh: 从书中选择一种交通工具（自行车、船、火车、汽车或飞机），并说明人们是如何让它随着时间变得越来越快的。

en: Name the vehicle and an early speed mentioned.
Describe one invention or change that increased its speed.
zh: 说出这种交通工具以及书中提到的一个早期速度。
描述一项使其提速的发明或改变。
